items.q: How do insulin and glucagon work together to regulate blood glucose?
items.correct: They are antagonistic — insulin lowers blood glucose (cells take up glucose, store as glycogen); glucagon raises it (glycogen broken back down to glucose).
items.misconception: Insulin and glucagon both lower blood glucose using different mechanisms.
items.random_wrong: Insulin works on muscles and glucagon works on the brain.
items.why: Students don't grasp 'antagonistic hormones' — they often think both hormones do similar jobs because both are about glucose.

items.q: What is the key difference between mitosis and meiosis?
items.correct: Mitosis produces two genetically identical diploid cells; meiosis produces four genetically different haploid cells (gametes).
items.misconception: Mitosis and meiosis both produce gametes; they are basically the same.
items.random_wrong: Mitosis happens in plants and meiosis happens in animals.
items.why: The similar names lead to confusion; students mix up which process produces gametes vs body cells.

items.q: Why is negative feedback the default mechanism in homeostasis?
items.correct: It opposes deviations from a set point, returning the variable toward its normal value — keeping internal conditions stable.
items.misconception: Negative feedback amplifies changes to make them more noticeable.
items.random_wrong: Negative feedback only operates when something goes wrong in the body.
items.why: The word 'negative' confuses students into thinking it amplifies/worsens, but in control theory 'negative' just means 'opposes the change'.

items.q: If image is 50 mm and real is 0.05 mm, magnification is:
items.why: Correct answer: ×1000. “×100” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which enzyme has an optimum pH near 2?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Pepsin. “Amylase” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: 30 cm³ gas in 60 s. Mean rate (cm³/s)?
items.why: The real figure is 0.5. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
